response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 10+ years of experience, but their core stack centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/GIS platforms — not C++. C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (Dec 2014–Jun 2015) writing C/C++ components for LabWindows/CVI. No demonstrated experience with STL-heavy modern C++, IEC 62443, industrial cybersecurity, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, or message-broker-based industrial automation. The seniority and engineering rigor are there, but the technology fit is weak for a C++-centric industrial-automation role.
strengths: 10+ years of senior engineering experience with strong software craftsmanship, TDD, and architectural skills
Distributed/high-performance systems background (low-latency DLang services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec)
Mentoring, workshop delivery, and technical communication track record (Selenium course, Web Internals workshop, conference talks)
concerns: Only ~6 months of professional C++ exposure, over a decade ago; no evidence of modern C++/STL mastery
No industrial-automation, IEC 62443/SDL, or industrial-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, MQTT)
Career trajectory is firmly web/cloud/TypeScript-oriented, suggesting low motivation and steep ramp-up for a C++ embedded-platform role

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering maturity and distributed-systems track record
